codeReview.summary: The feature correctly adds activated_at persistence and newest-first ordering for /coins/list/new, and it preserves list/search/detail continuity for discovered ids. However, it does not add regression coverage or implementation evidence that discovered ids remain usable through /coins/{id}/history, /market_chart, and /ohlc as required by VAL-CROSS-002, and the tracker statement overclaims that history continuity is covered.

issues.description: The new duplicate-discovery test stops at /coins/list/new and direct DB inspection, but never follows the discovered id through /coins/{id}/history, /coins/{id}/market_chart, or /coins/{id}/ohlc. The existing continuity test added earlier only checks list/search/detail surfaces, so this feature lacks feature-attributable evidence for VAL-CROSS-002's required historical-surface continuity.

The tracker says /coins/list/new ids are reusable across list/search/detail/history surfaces, but the committed tests and diff only verify list/search/detail. That wording currently overstates the evidence shipped in this feature.

summary: Reviewed handoff, transcript skeleton, skill, architecture guidance, and commit 4f97852fb1c65a951c6012a8e28785d33a9b9208. New-listing ordering and duplicate-discovery persistence look correct, but the feature does not demonstrate or regress historical-surface continuity for discovered ids, so it does not fully satisfy the assigned VAL-CROSS-002 review focus.
